ops.title: cc-guides shell-artifact adoption recipe — test.sh + gen-version-xcconfig.sh (with POSIX gotcha)
ops.body:
Recon (2026-07-19) for making the fleet's shared shell scripts cc-guides-managed artifacts from cc-skills. Mechanism CONFIRMED; one blocking edit flagged.

## Mechanism (verified against cc-guides main + cc-skills)
- Shell artifacts ship today: `KindSH` (guide/kind.go:15-27); layout dir name IS the artifact path, so `scripts/test.sh` -> `.claude/fragments/scripts/test.sh/layout.toml` (guide/artifact.go:9-37). KindSH: ext `.sh`, strategy=append, shebang:true, newMode 0o755 (exec bit on create), lint rules shShebang+shNoMustache (guide/spec.go:73-82).
- Imports: `cc-skills:<name>` -> `cc-skills/plugin/guides/sh/<name>.sh` (source/resolver.go:130). Per-artifact-dir `[sources.cc-skills] source = "github:yasyf/cc-skills@main"`.
- Proof-of-pattern self-hosted in cc-skills: `plugins/slop-cop/scripts/install-binary.sh` composed from `cc-skills:install-binary-latest` with `args = {...}` token substitution.

## 480s/600s test.sh divergence: SOLVED by args, NOT unification
The two test.sh copies differ ONLY in `-timeout 480s` (daemonkit) vs `-timeout 600s` (fusekit); else byte-identical. Shared body carries `-timeout {{timeout}}`; each layout.toml passes `args = { timeout = "480s"|"600s" }`. Compose cannot vary a mid-body line by joining fragments (guide/compose.go:34-71 is append-only; only first piece carries `#!`), so token substitution is the only mechanism.

## gen-version-xcconfig.sh: byte-identical daemonkit <-> captain-hook/helper/ -> single unparametrized shared fragment, no args.

## BLOCKING EDIT (gate decision): POSIX rewrite required
cc-guides sh-lint (cc-skills CI .github/workflows/guides-lint.yml runs `cc-guides lint plugin/guides/`) requires every shared sh fragment to literally start `#!/bin/sh` and be POSIX. Both scripts declare `#!/usr/bin/env bash` + `set -euo pipefail`, and test.sh uses `$(( 10#${cur} + headroom ))` -- the `10#` explicit-base prefix is bash/ksh, NOT POSIX (dash rejects). Adoption REQUIRES rewriting test.sh to strict POSIX: `#!/bin/sh`; `set -eu` (pipefail inert -- the one pipe ps|awk has an explicit `[ -n "${cur:-}" ] || cur=0` fallback); `10#${cur}` -> POSIX leading-zero strip `cur=${cur#"${cur%%[!0]*}"}; [ -n "$cur" ] || cur=0`. This CHANGES bytes, so the plan's "byte-faithful" check is impossible -> replace with BEHAVIORAL EQUIVALENCE (run rendered test.sh, confirm ulimit -u cap computes identically; the fork-bomb guard is load-bearing, AGENTS.md Testing / 2026-06-24 incident). gen-version-xcconfig.sh has the same shebang/pipefail mismatch but no pipes -> dropping pipefail is a no-op (lower risk).

## Rollout (drift caution)
1. Land the two POSIX-clean fragments on cc-skills main. cc-skills pinned at a646e9b in daemonkit's cc-guides.lock.
2. A SCOPED `cc-guides render <path>` reuses the existing pin (won't see new fragments); only a FULL unscoped render re-resolves aliases to cc-skills HEAD and rewrites the lock -- RISKS pulling unrelated cc-skills drift into daemonkit/fusekit AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md. Diff cc-skills main vs a646e9b first; review the render diff so only script targets change.
3. Delete hand-written scripts/test.sh + templates/gen-version-xcconfig.sh; `cc-guides render ... --force` (no GENERATED marker -> v3Overwritable refuses without --force).
4. `cc-guides check` clean; CI green.

captain-hook's gen-version-xcconfig.sh copy = handoff note only (never touch captain-hook this session). Wider fleet sweep = follow-up task.
